political.s: FEC: $45K PAC donations; 67% to Republican, 33% to Democratic committees.  [CA CalAccess + NY COELIG + TX Ethics adds state-level totals.]

charity.s: $350M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.70% of revenue) via Goldman Sachs Gives. Operates the Goldman Sachs Foundation: $44.5M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.focus: Economic growth, community development, underserved communities

environment.s: Banking on Climate Chaos 2024: $159.5B in fossil-fuel financing 2016-2023 (#14 globally).

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

privacy.s: CFPB consumer complaints: 19428 on record (100 in past 12mo); 100% timely response rate.

execPay.s: UK FCA fine for 1MDB-related controls (2020-10-22). £96.6M in fines.

wiki.parent: The Vanguard Group, BlackRock, State Street Corporation
wiki.industry: financial services, financial service activities, except insurance and pension funding, financial sector, International Standard Industrial Classification
wiki.controversies:
== Controversies ==

Goldman Sachs has been embroiled in numerous controversies since its founding. The bank has faced particular criticism for its role in the 2008 global financial crisis and, more recently, in the 1MDB scandal in Malaysia.
wiki.extract: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. is an American multinational investment bank and financial services company. Founded in 1869, Goldman Sachs is headquartered in the Battery Park City neighborhood of Manhattan in New York City, with regional offices in many international financial centers. Goldman Sachs is one of the largest investment banks in the world by revenue and is ranked 32nd on the Fortune 500 list of the largest United States corporations by total revenue. In the Forbes Global 2000 of 2025, Goldman Sachs ranked 20th. It is considered a systemically important financial institution by the
